_template._comment: Copy this block, rename the key to your company slug (lowercase, hyphen-separated). Every field is optional — only the ones you set will override the default site. Leave a field out to keep the default.
_template.displayName: Shown in 'Why X should work with me?' and the tab title. Defaults to the ?company= param as-typed.
_template.heroHeadline:
Main h1 in the hero. Use
for a line break. Keep to 2 short lines.
_template.heroSubtext: The paragraph under the hero headline. Tailor to what this company cares about.
_template.ctaTitle: Final 'Call me, maybe?' heading. Default is 'Call me, maybe?'
_template.ctaDesc: Final CTA description. Default is 'No pitch. No pressure. Just a quick conversation to see if I can help.'
_personalNote_recipe: Locked structure for each company's 'Why I'm writing to you, {COMPANY}' card.
Title is always: 'Why I'm writing to you, {COMPANY}.'
Bullets are locked — only swap the body, not the title:
1. 'I've been watching how you build.'  → name ONE specific real thing (campaign, product, cultural choice). End with: that's what I'd want to be close to.
2. 'Your ambition is a different kind of operating problem.'  → flatter with a real number/target. End with: I'd love to understand how the team keeps up.
3. 'My range might fit here.'  → connect one of Tanya's 7 careers or BigCo chapters to their work. Humble.
Rules: 25–40 words per body. Warm, curious, respectful. No LinkedIn-speak. No emoji. No diagnosing problems. No '90-day plans'. No 'I've done my homework' — the details prove it. Contractions fine.
Subtle = naming a real campaign/number/detail. Not subtle = restating the thesis or sounding cocky.
Wrap words in … for coral italic accent (same as the 'you' in the title).

personalNote.eyebrow: A LITTLE NOTE JUST FOR {COMPANY} — small uppercase label.
personalNote.title: Why I'm writing to you, {COMPANY}.
personalNote.intro: Short 1-sentence setup for the bullets. Keep it honest. e.g. 'Before I send anything over — three things I've been thinking about.'

points.title: I've been watching how you build.
{their ambition} is a different kind of operating problem.
My range might fit here.

2070health.ctaDesc: No pitch. No pressure. 20 minutes to see if I'm the program manager your special-projects bench is missing.

personalNote.eyebrow: A little note just for 2070Health
personalNote.title: Why I'm writing to you, 2070Health.
personalNote.intro: Before I send anything over — three things I've been thinking about.

points.title: I've been watching how you build.
points.body: Building healthcare ventures from the platform in instead of the thesis out — a 0→1 studio model for a sector that usually waits for white papers — is the kind of operator call I'd want to be close to.

points.title: Your ambition is a different kind of operating problem.
points.body: Every special project has to ship like a startup while reporting like a platform. That's two operating rhythms at once. I'd love to understand how the team keeps up.

points.title: My range might fit here.
points.body: Six years at Microsoft & Uber shipping for coworkers and strangers alike. Then seven creative careers learning to run small things end to end. Special projects needs both muscles.

accelerate-indian-philanthropy.ctaDesc: No pitch. No pressure. 20 minutes to see if the founders' office work here could use another operator.

personalNote.eyebrow: A little note just for AIP
personalNote.title: Why I'm writing to you, Accelerate Indian Philanthropy.
personalNote.intro: Before I send anything over — three things I've been thinking about.

points.title: I've been watching how you build.
points.body: Turning philanthropy from a cheque into a community of givers who learn from each other — that's a much harder operating problem than it looks, and the part I'd want to be close to.

points.title: Your ambition is a different kind of operating problem.
points.body: India's UHNI cohort is small, private, and bespoke by default. Running executive ops for a cohort like that means every programme is a custom build. I'd love to understand how the team keeps up.

points.title: My range might fit here.
points.body: Six years at Microsoft & Uber running programmes at scale. Then seven creative careers — including a Bangalore community club where strangers had to leave as friends. Programmes that land on giving run on that same muscle.

astra-security.heroHeadline:
Security doesn't ship
unless a generalist makes it ship.
astra-security.heroSubtext: Ex-Microsoft, ex-Uber. Six years shipping planet-scale infra, then 18 months of seven creative careers learning to build from zero. Both muscles are useful in a founder's office.
astra-security.ctaDesc: No pitch. No pressure. 20 minutes to see if I'm the generalist your founding team is looking for.

personalNote.eyebrow: A little note just for Astra
personalNote.title: Why I'm writing to you, Astra Security.
personalNote.intro: Before I send anything over — three things I've been thinking about.

points.title: I've been watching how you build.
points.body: Turning pentesting from a scary black box into a dashboard that a non-security founder can actually use — that's a storytelling choice as much as a product one, and the part I'd want to be close to.

points.title: Your ambition is a different kind of operating problem.
points.body: Protecting 10,000+ businesses without a dedicated security team at each means you're not selling a product — you're selling a substitute. I'd love to understand how the team keeps up.

points.title: My range might fit here.
points.body: Six years at Microsoft on product activation — the invisible infra that 'only breaks.' Then seven creative careers running things end to end. Security is invisible until it isn't — I know that shape.

biome-build.ctaDesc: No pitch. No pressure. 20 minutes to see if my mix of design and ops is the bridge your founder's office needs.

personalNote.eyebrow: A little note just for Biome/Build
personalNote.title: Why I'm writing to you, Biome/Build.
personalNote.intro: Before I send anything over — three things I've been thinking about.

points.title: I've been watching how you build.
points.body: Putting a design-native seat inside the founder's office — not as decoration, as decision-making — is the kind of structural call I'd want to be close to watching play out.

points.title: Your ambition is a different kind of operating problem.
points.body: Design and strategy usually move at different tempos. Making them share one rhythm in an early-stage team is a real operating challenge. I'd love to understand how the team keeps up.

points.title: My range might fit here.
points.body: Engineer by training, designer by nature, operator by the last six years. Then seven creative careers — mural, exhibition, event design. The whole shape sits in the seam between design and strategy.

bitespeed.ctaDesc: No pitch. No pressure. 20 minutes to see if an ex-Uber generalist is useful in your FP&A seat.

personalNote.eyebrow: A little note just for BiteSpeed
personalNote.title: Why I'm writing to you, BiteSpeed.
personalNote.intro: Before I send anything over — three things I've been thinking about.

points.title: I've been watching how you build.
points.body: Betting that commerce in India happens on WhatsApp — not a website, not an app — is the kind of channel call the whole product flows from. That's what I'd want to be close to.

points.title: Your ambition is a different kind of operating problem.
points.body: YC-backed, D2C-heavy book, charging per-message at Shopify-brand scale. FP&A at that shape is half pricing, half psychology. I'd love to understand how the team keeps up.

points.title: My range might fit here.
points.body: Uber taught me that FP&A lives or dies on how the data pipelines are shaped. I built those pipelines. Also telemarketed on Flipkart Live, so I've watched conversational commerce from the ground up.

blissclub.heroHeadline:
Activewear brands live or die
on how they run their OKRs.
blissclub.heroSubtext: Ex-Microsoft, ex-Uber. Six years shipping at scale. Then 18 months of seven creative careers — florist, event lead, community host — learning to build small things that actually move.
blissclub.ctaDesc: No pitch. No pressure. 20 minutes to see if I'm the Chief of Staff your next chapter needs.

personalNote.eyebrow: A little note just for Blissclub
personalNote.title: Why I'm writing to you, Blissclub.
personalNote.intro: Before I send anything over — three things I've been thinking about.

points.title: I've been watching how you build.
points.body: Designing an activewear brand around movement that real Indian women actually do — not the Lululemon aspirational fantasy — is the cultural call I'd want to be close to.

points.title: Your ambition is a different kind of operating problem.
points.body: India's fastest-growing activewear brand means growing faster than your supply chain, your hiring plan, and your OKR system can keep up. I'd love to understand how the team keeps up.

points.title: My range might fit here.
points.body: Six years at Microsoft & Uber running OKRs at scale. Then seven creative careers — including running a Bangalore community club. Chief of Staff at a community-led brand lives in that exact seam.

bold-cap.heroHeadline:
A creator who executes like an operator

bold-cap.heroSubtext: Ex-Microsoft, ex-Uber. Six years of scaling. Then 18 months of seven creative careers — mural, content, community — learning to make storytelling and shipping sit in the same person.
bold-cap.ctaDesc: No pitch. No pressure. 20 minutes to see if the creator + operator mix is what you're hiring for.

personalNote.eyebrow: A little note just for Bold Cap
personalNote.title: Why I'm writing to you, Bold Cap.
personalNote.intro: Before I send anything over — three things I've been thinking about.

points.title: I've been watching how you build.
points.body: Hiring a creator-operator for the Chief of Staff seat — not a banker, not a consultant — is the call I'd want to be close to watching. It says something about what you think a platform is.

points.title: Your ambition is a different kind of operating problem.
points.body: Platform VC means you're running a content engine, a founder service, and a portfolio at the same time. That's three operating rhythms in one seat. I'd love to understand how the team keeps up.

points.title: My range might fit here.
points.body: I'm 339K followers on Instagram, mid-way through seven creative careers, and six years of Microsoft + Uber behind that. Creator who executes like an operator sounds like the shape.

A food brand's creative work

california-burrito.ctaDesc: No pitch. No pressure. 20 minutes to see if I'm the Creative Head your kitchens are missing.

personalNote.eyebrow: A little note just for California Burrito
personalNote.title: Why I'm writing to you, California Burrito.
personalNote.intro: Before I send anything over — three things I've been thinking about.

points.title: I've been watching how you build.
points.body: Growing from a single Bangalore outlet into a Series B consumer brand without losing the thing that made the burritos feel specific — that's an identity-preservation problem as much as a scaling one, and the part I'd want to be close to.

points.title: Your ambition is a different kind of operating problem.
points.body: Series B, Elevation-backed, creative leadership that has to translate across kitchens, packaging, reels, and stores. I'd love to understand how the team keeps up.

points.title: My range might fit here.

certifyme.ctaDesc: No pitch. No pressure. 20 minutes to see if I'm the generalist your growth bench is missing.

points.title: I've been watching how you build.

cloudsek.ctaDesc: No pitch. No pressure. 20 minutes to see if an ex-Microsoft generalist is useful next to the founding team.

personalNote.eyebrow: A little note just for CloudSEK
personalNote.title: Why I'm writing to you, CloudSEK.
personalNote.intro: Before I send anything over — three things I've been thinking about.

points.title: I've been watching how you build.
points.body: Using AI to watch the internet for your customers' exposures — scraping dark web and leaks at scale — is a product that only makes sense if you can tell the story simply. That's the part I'd want to be close to.

points.title: Your ambition is a different kind of operating problem.
points.body: AI cybersecurity means a moving threat model, a moving regulatory floor, and a moving pricing model. Founder's office has to hold all three. I'd love to understand how the team keeps up.

points.title: My range might fit here.

contineu.ctaDesc: No pitch. No pressure. 20 minutes to see if I'm the generalist your founding team is hiring for.

personalNote.eyebrow: A little note just for Contineu

personalNote.intro: Before I send anything over — three things I've been thinking about.

points.title: I've been watching how you build.
points.body: Early-stage founder's office isn't a seat — it's whatever's on fire that week. Hiring for range instead of specialty is the bet I'd want to be close to.

points.title: Your ambition is a different kind of operating problem.

points.title: My range might fit here.

cube.ctaDesc: No pitch. No pressure. 20 minutes to see if I'm useful next to the founding team.

personalNote.eyebrow: A little note just for Cube
personalNote.title: Why I'm writing to you, Cube.
personalNote.intro: Before I send anything over — three things I've been thinking about.

points.title: I've been watching how you build.
points.body: Making marketing ops feel like a product instead of a spreadsheet is the cultural call marketing stacks rarely get right. That's what I'd want to be close to.

points.title: Your ambition is a different kind of operating problem.

points.title: My range might fit here.

curefit.heroHeadline:
A fitness brand's story

curefit.heroSubtext: Ex-Microsoft, ex-Uber. Six years shipping at scale. Then 18 months of seven creative careers — mural, event, community host — learning to build things people feel, not just see.

personalNote.eyebrow: A little note just for CureFit

points.title: I've been watching how you build.

points.title: Your ambition is a different kind of operating problem.

points.title: My range might fit here.

deconstruct.heroHeadline:
A skincare brand's creative

personalNote.eyebrow: A little note just for Deconstruct
personalNote.title: Why I'm writing to you, Deconstruct.
personalNote.intro: Before I send anything over — three things I've been thinking about.

points.title: I've been watching how you build.
points.body: Selling skincare by actives, not adjectives — the 'niacinamide 10%' instead of 'glow potion' move — is a real category bet. That's what I'd want to be close to.

points.title: Your ambition is a different kind of operating problem.
points.body: D2C skincare at the stage of new-product development, positioning, and campaign calendar all at once is three operating rhythms in one brand seat. I'd love to understand how the team keeps up.

points.title: My range might fit here.

digitide.ctaDesc: No pitch. No pressure. 20 minutes to see if an ex-BigCo + creative-generalist mix is what you're hiring for.

personalNote.eyebrow: A little note just for Digitide

points.title: I've been watching how you build.
points.body: Running a 4000+ person tech company from a Chief of Staff seat means protecting the CEO's attention is half the job. The discipline of that is the part I'd want to be close to.

points.title: Your ambition is a different kind of operating problem.

points.title: My range might fit here.
points.body: Microsoft: 220,000+ employees. Uber: 30,000+. I've been the edge-case ticket in rooms that size. Then seven creative careers taught me how small things actually ship. Chief of Staff needs both muscles.

points.title: Your ambition is a different kind of operating problem.

points.title: My range might fit here.
points.body: Six years at Microsoft & Uber running ops inside product teams. Then seven creative careers — including building an Instagram audience of 339K people by being honest. Media + ops is most of what I've been doing.

elevation-capital.heroHeadline:
Chief of Staff at a VC firm

elevation-capital.heroSubtext: Ex-Microsoft, ex-Uber. Six years of shipping. Then 18 months of seven creative careers — mural, community host, event marketer, live commerce. 30 portfolio companies means 30 different operating problems. I've been 7 of them.
elevation-capital.ctaDesc: No pitch. No pressure. 20 minutes to see if a generalist who's lived the 0→1 side is the Chief of Staff you want.

personalNote.eyebrow: A little note just for Elevation
personalNote.title: Why I'm writing to you, Elevation Capital.
personalNote.intro: Before I send anything over — three things I've been thinking about.

points.title: I've been watching how you build.
points.body: Investing across Swiggy, Meesho, Paytm and still backing pre-seed consumer bets is a portfolio temperament I'd want to be close to — most firms pick one personality.

points.title: Your ambition is a different kind of operating problem.
points.body: A Chief of Staff at a VC firm is running IR, portfolio support, and partner calendars — three founders' offices stacked on top of each other. I'd love to understand how the team keeps up.

points.title: My range might fit here.
points.body: Six years at Microsoft & Uber. Then seven unrelated creative careers. I've lived the 0→1 founder side of what Elevation invests in. That's the language a Chief of Staff needs to speak with 30 founders.

enterpret.ctaTitle: Let's talk Chief of Staff to the CTO.
enterpret.ctaDesc: No pitch. No pressure. 20 minutes to see if an ex-Uber data-pipelines person + creative generalist is useful next to the CTO.

personalNote.eyebrow: A little note just for Enterpret
personalNote.title: Why I'm writing to you, Enterpret.
personalNote.intro: Before I send anything over — three things I've been thinking about.

points.title: I've been watching how you build.
points.body: Making customer feedback a structured data source — not a Notion doc the PM skims — is the kind of category-creating bet I'd want to be close to watching mature.

points.title: Your ambition is a different kind of operating problem.
points.body: Chief of Staff to a CTO means translating between product, engineering, and customer reality in three different vocabularies. I'd love to understand how the team keeps up.

points.title: My range might fit here.
points.body: At Uber I built data pipelines for internal developers — my customers were coworkers. That's the exact muscle a CTO's Chief of Staff runs on, minus the coworkers.

points.title: I've been watching how you build.

points.title: Your ambition is a different kind of operating problem.

points.title: My range might fit here.
points.body: Mural, exhibition design, event marketer, community host, live commerce, florist. Every one of those is 'brand by doing.' That's the seed-stage shape of a brand lead.

exotel.heroHeadline:
Founder DNA isn't a title.

exotel.heroSubtext: Ex-Microsoft, ex-Uber. Six years shipping at scale. Then 18 months of seven creative careers — quitting, no plan B, figuring it out by doing. That's not a hobby; that's founder DNA.
exotel.ctaDesc: No pitch. No pressure. 20 minutes to see if the ex-founder shape you're looking for is closer to mine than you'd think.

personalNote.eyebrow: A little note just for Exotel
personalNote.title: Why I'm writing to you, Exotel.
personalNote.intro: Before I send anything over — three things I've been thinking about.

points.title: I've been watching how you build.
points.body: Staying a platform instead of drifting into point solutions, after 15+ years of Indian communications shifting under you, is the kind of patience I'd want to be close to.

points.title: Your ambition is a different kind of operating problem.
points.body: A platform serving banks, delivery apps, and healthcare on the same cloud voice backbone means the founder's office has to hold three customer realities at once. I'd love to understand how the team keeps up.

points.title: My range might fit here.
points.body: Didn't found a company, but I did quit a crore-paying job to try 50 creative careers with no plan B. Four months, seven careers, still going. That's the DNA, even if the resume line looks different.

flent.ctaDesc: No pitch. No pressure. 20 minutes to see if I'm the generalist your founding team is looking for.

personalNote.eyebrow: A little note just for Flent
personalNote.title: Why I'm writing to you, Flent.
personalNote.intro: Before I send anything over — three things I've been thinking about.

points.title: I've been watching how you build.
points.body: Choosing to run the founder's office as the engine room of growth — not a strategy studio that writes memos — is the operating call I'd want to be close to.

points.title: Your ambition is a different kind of operating problem.
points.body: Early-stage means every initiative is also a hiring decision, a comms decision, and a finance decision in disguise. I'd love to understand how the team keeps up.

points.title: My range might fit here.
points.body: Six years at Microsoft & Uber shipping things coworkers depended on. Then seven creative careers — show up, learn fast, finish the thing. Founder's office, on most days, is exactly that.

good-monk.ctaDesc: No pitch. No pressure. 20 minutes to see if I'm the generalist your superfoods team is hiring for.

personalNote.eyebrow: A little note just for GOOD MONK
personalNote.title: Why I'm writing to you, GOOD MONK.
personalNote.intro: Before I send anything over — three things I've been thinking about.

points.title: I've been watching how you build.
points.body: Reframing superfoods from wellness jargon into something Indian parents actually want to buy for their kids is the cultural call that matters most. That's what I'd want to be close to.

points.title: Your ambition is a different kind of operating problem.

points.title: My range might fit here.
points.body: Florist — I know what building a product-as-ritual looks like at ground level. Event marketer for a food chain. Telemarketer on Flipkart Live. Consumer brand operating is most of what I've done the last 18 months.

gloroots.ctaDesc: No pitch. No pressure. 20 minutes to see if an ex-Microsoft, ex-Uber engineer with a generalist streak is useful here.

personalNote.eyebrow: A little note just for Gloroots
personalNote.title: Why I'm writing to you, Gloroots.
personalNote.intro: Before I send anything over — three things I've been thinking about.

points.title: I've been watching how you build.
points.body: Putting an engineer inside the founder's office — not only building product but shaping how the company thinks — is the cultural choice I'd want to be close to.

points.title: Your ambition is a different kind of operating problem.
points.body: Global EOR + payroll means compliance in 100+ jurisdictions, each changing quarterly. The engineering surface area at that shape is relentless. I'd love to understand how the team keeps up.

points.title: My range might fit here.
points.body: Microsoft: product activation infra at planet scale. Uber: data pipelines for internal devs. Then seven creative careers — a reminder that engineering shows up everywhere if you let it.

goodscore.ctaDesc: No pitch. No pressure. 20 minutes to see if I'm the generalist your founders are hiring for.

personalNote.eyebrow: A little note just for GoodScore
personalNote.title: Why I'm writing to you, GoodScore.
personalNote.intro: Before I send anything over — three things I've been thinking about.

points.title: I've been watching how you build.

points.title: Your ambition is a different kind of operating problem.

points.title: My range might fit here.

hector-beverages.heroHeadline:
A nostalgia brand's copy
is its second recipe.
hector-beverages.heroSubtext: Ex-Microsoft, ex-Uber. Six years shipping at scale. Then 18 months of seven creative careers — mural, event, community host, live commerce. I know what storytelling feels like when people can touch it.
hector-beverages.ctaDesc: No pitch. No pressure. 20 minutes to see if a creative copywriter with range fits your table.

personalNote.eyebrow: A little note just for Paper Boat
personalNote.title: Why I'm writing to you, Hector Beverages.
personalNote.intro: Before I send anything over — three things I've been thinking about.

points.title: I've been watching how you build.
points.body: Keeping Paper Boat's voice the same — from the first aamras pack to today's shelves — while adding new formats and drinks is the discipline I'd want to be close to. Most nostalgic brands slide in ten years.

points.title: Your ambition is a different kind of operating problem.
points.body: FMCG copy at Indian scale has to land in a print ad, a 6-second reel, and a kirana shelf within the same week. I'd love to understand how the team keeps up.

points.title: My range might fit here.

points.body: Bewakoof's #AllEyesOnYou is a clinic in community-led work — but it's the scaffolding around each founder that's harder and more interesting. That's the part I'd want to be close to.

points.title: Your ambition is a different kind of operating problem.
points.body: 30+ brands, each with its own founder, category, and stage. Platform team has to keep up with that many operating rhythms. I'd love to understand how the team keeps up.

points.title: My range might fit here.
points.body: Six years at Microsoft & Uber. Eighteen months running seven unrelated careers. A House of Brands is one of the few places where 'I've done a bit of everything' reads like a fit, not a flag.

third-wave-coffee.heroHeadline:
A coffee brand
is a café AND a shelf.
third-wave-coffee.heroSubtext: Ex-Microsoft, ex-Uber. Six years shipping at scale. Then 18 months of seven creative careers — florist, mural, event, community host. Consumer brand is where all of that converges.
third-wave-coffee.ctaTitle: Let's build Third Wave's FMCG next chapter.
third-wave-coffee.ctaDesc: No pitch. No pressure. 20 minutes to see if I'm the Brand Manager your shelf portfolio needs.

personalNote.eyebrow: A little note just for Third Wave
personalNote.title: Why I'm writing to you, Third Wave Coffee.
personalNote.intro: Before I send anything over — three things I've been thinking about.

points.title: I've been watching how you build.
points.body: Going from a cafe brand to an FMCG brand without breaking what made the cafes feel specific is a brand-translation problem that most cafe chains don't survive. That's what I'd want to be close to.

points.title: Your ambition is a different kind of operating problem.
points.body: Building an FMCG portfolio off a cafe brand means shelf, cafe, and d2c all telling the same story, at three very different unit economics. I'd love to understand how the team keeps up.

points.title: My range might fit here.
points.body: Florist — where I learned cold-chain and vendor reality. Community host — where 'the room' was the product. Mural — where the work had to survive in a specific place. FMCG brand lives in all three.

truefoundry.ctaDesc: No pitch. No pressure. 20 minutes to see if an ex-Uber infra person + creative generalist fits your GTM motion.

personalNote.eyebrow: A little note just for TrueFoundry
personalNote.title: Why I'm writing to you, TrueFoundry.
personalNote.intro: Before I send anything over — three things I've been thinking about.

points.title: I've been watching how you build.
points.body: Building ML infra for teams who think of themselves as developers, not data scientists, is a positioning move that decides the whole GTM. That's the part I'd want to be close to.

points.title: Your ambition is a different kind of operating problem.
points.body: ML platform sales mean you're being compared against 'build it yourself' every single meeting. GTM has to show ROI in weeks. I'd love to understand how the team keeps up.

points.title: My range might fit here.
points.body: Uber: I built data pipelines for internal developers — selling by being useful to peers. Same GTM posture, smaller stage. Plus seven creative careers where each one was a miniature GTM test.

unifize.ctaDesc: No pitch. No pressure. 20 minutes to see if a generalist-operator fits the sales ops seat.

personalNote.eyebrow: A little note just for Unifize
personalNote.title: Why I'm writing to you, Unifize.
personalNote.intro: Before I send anything over — three things I've been thinking about.

points.title: I've been watching how you build.
points.body: Building a manufacturing collaboration platform for a category that still runs on email chains and WhatsApp groups is the kind of patient category-making I'd want to be close to.

points.title: Your ambition is a different kind of operating problem.
points.body: Manufacturing SaaS sales mean long cycles, multiple stakeholders, and a buyer who's been burned by enterprise software before. Sales ops is where those frictions surface. I'd love to understand how the team keeps up.

points.title: My range might fit here.
points.body: Microsoft: enterprise product activation and the pipelines around it. Six years of that. Then seven creative careers, several of which were small-scale sales ops in disguise.

varsal-terra.ctaDesc: No pitch. No pressure. 20 minutes to see if a generalist with a learning streak fits the clinical-chain seat.

personalNote.eyebrow: A little note just for Varsal Terra
personalNote.title: Why I'm writing to you, Varsal Terra.
personalNote.intro: Before I send anything over — three things I've been thinking about.

points.title: I've been watching how you build.
points.body: Running a specialty clinical chain where each location is both a P&L and a care experience is a structural bet that only pays back with patient operating discipline. That's what I'd want to be close to.

points.title: Your ambition is a different kind of operating problem.
points.body: Healthcare chains live on clinician trust, insurer contracts, and patient outcomes — three clocks that don't match. Chief of Staff at the founder's office has to hold all three. I'd love to understand how the team keeps up.

points.title: My range might fit here.
points.body: Six years at Microsoft & Uber in programs where trust and ops lived in the same room. Then seven creative careers learning fast in new categories. Clinical chain is a new one — but not a different shape.

weekday.ctaDesc: No pitch. No pressure. 20 minutes to see if I'm the generalist your YC-shape team is hiring for.

personalNote.eyebrow: A little note just for Weekday
personalNote.title: Why I'm writing to you, Weekday.
personalNote.intro: Before I send anything over — three things I've been thinking about.

points.title: I've been watching how you build.
points.body: Running a hiring platform where candidates come through other candidates — referral as the whole funnel — is a structural bet on people wanting to work with people they already trust. That's the part I'd want to be close to.

points.title: Your ambition is a different kind of operating problem.
points.body: Referral density is a chicken-and-egg problem every week. Founder's office at a YC-shape team is where that math is fought out. I'd love to understand how the team keeps up.

points.title: My range might fit here.
points.body: Six years at Microsoft & Uber building things my coworkers depended on. Then seven creative careers — half of which found me through other people. I know the referral side of this intimately.

weekday-ai.ctaDesc: No pitch. No pressure. 20 minutes to see if I'm the GTM generalist your YC-shape team is hiring for.

personalNote.eyebrow: A little note just for Weekday AI
personalNote.title: Why I'm writing to you, Weekday AI.
personalNote.intro: Before I send anything over — three things I've been thinking about.

points.title: I've been watching how you build.
points.body: Layering AI on a referral-hiring platform — instead of rebuilding the category from scratch — is the kind of focused second-act bet I'd want to be close to.

points.title: Your ambition is a different kind of operating problem.
points.body: GTM for an AI hiring product means you're pitching to recruiters who have seen 50 AI pitches. Standing out is not a demo problem; it's a trust problem. I'd love to understand how the team keeps up.

points.title: My range might fit here.
points.body: Six years at Microsoft & Uber translating technical product to non-technical buyers. Then seven creative careers where trust was the whole conversion. GTM / growth at your shape lives there.

xoxoday.ctaTitle: Let's talk Chief of Staff / COO.
xoxoday.ctaDesc: No pitch. No pressure. 20 minutes to see if an ops-native generalist is useful to the COO.

personalNote.eyebrow: A little note just for Xoxoday
personalNote.title: Why I'm writing to you, Xoxoday.
personalNote.intro: Before I send anything over — three things I've been thinking about.

points.title: I've been watching how you build.
points.body: Building a rewards & incentives platform that works across gifting, employee recognition, and channel partners — three very different buyers on one stack — is an operational call the whole company runs on. That's what I'd want to be close to.

points.title: Your ambition is a different kind of operating problem.
points.body: Chief of Staff partnering with a COO means protecting attention across functions that all need the same 45 minutes every week. I'd love to understand how the team keeps up.

points.title: My range might fit here.
points.body: Six years at Microsoft & Uber running programs across multiple customer types. Then seven creative careers managing my own rewards, time, and deliverables. Chief of Staff to a COO is the grown-up version.

zamp.ctaDesc: No pitch. No pressure. 20 minutes to see if I'm the generalist your founder's office is hiring for.

personalNote.eyebrow: A little note just for Zamp
personalNote.title: Why I'm writing to you, Zamp.
personalNote.intro: Before I send anything over — three things I've been thinking about.

points.title: I've been watching how you build.
points.body: Running a finance infrastructure company means the product has to feel steady while the regulatory floor moves. Treating that as a strategy problem — not a compliance afterthought — is the part I'd want to be close to.

points.title: Your ambition is a different kind of operating problem.
points.body: Finance infra buyers are CFOs and controllers — a buyer who resents surprises. Strategy + ops at founder's office has to keep the company fast and the customer calm. I'd love to understand how the team keeps up.

points.title: My range might fit here.
points.body: Microsoft & Uber — infra products where shipping fast and shipping safe were the same sentence. Then seven creative careers. Finance infra founder's office is the same shape, one industry over.

famyo.heroHeadline:
A character-led D2C brand
needs a character-led generalist.
famyo.heroSubtext: Ex-Microsoft, ex-Uber. Seven creative careers in 18 months — mural artist, florist, event lead, community host, live-commerce host. I know how to make something kids remember and parents buy.
famyo.ctaDesc: No pitch deck. No 20-slide intro. Just a real conversation about what you need and whether I can ship it.

personalNote.eyebrow: A little note just for Famyo
personalNote.title: Why I'm writing to you, Famyo.
personalNote.intro: Before I send anything over — three things I've been thinking about.

points.title: I've been watching how you build.
points.body: The choice to lead with characters, not SKUs — turning everyday kid essentials into things kids actually ask their parents for — is the kind of product instinct I'd want to be close to.

points.title: Your ambition is a different kind of operating problem.
points.body: 40% QoQ growth on a ₹12 Cr ARR, in a kids-essentials category that's barely been built in India. I'd love to understand how the team keeps up.

points.title: My range might fit here.
points.body: Six years at Microsoft & Uber, then seven unrelated creative careers in 18 months — mural, exhibition design, live-commerce. A character-led D2C brand might be one of the few places that shape reads like a fit.

goodera.heroHeadline:
Scaling good is the kind
of operating problem I'd show up for.
goodera.heroSubtext: Ex-Microsoft, ex-Uber. Six years of scaling programs at companies built for scale. Then 18 months trying seven creative careers — including a Bangalore community club where strangers had to leave as friends. That's the range Chief of Staff at Goodera probably wants.
goodera.ctaDesc: No pitch. No pressure. 20 minutes to see if I'm the generalist your Founder's Office is looking for.

personalNote.eyebrow: A little note just for Goodera
personalNote.title: Why I'm writing to you, Goodera.
personalNote.intro: Before I send anything over — three things I've been thinking about.

points.title: I've been watching how you build.
points.body: Hosting Karma Summit Asia with 150+ companies in one room — turning a category into a community — is exactly the kind of move I'd want to be close to.

points.title: Your ambition is a different kind of operating problem.
points.body: 65+ Fortune 500 clients, a network of 50,000 nonprofits across 100 countries, and 100% YoY growth. I'd love to understand how the team keeps up.

points.title: My range might fit here.
points.body: Six years at Microsoft & Uber, then seven creative careers — including hosting a Bangalore community club where strangers had to leave as friends. A volunteering platform runs on that same muscle.

homelane.heroHeadline:
Design doesn't live in a brief.
It lives in the room.
homelane.heroSubtext: Ex-Microsoft, ex-Uber. Seven creative careers in 18 months — mural, exhibition design, event design. I've shipped design where people can touch it, walk through it, remember it. That's HomeLane's whole thing.
homelane.ctaDesc: No pitch. No pressure. 20 minutes to see if I'm the generalist your creative team is missing.

personalNote.eyebrow: A little note just for HomeLane
personalNote.title: Why I'm writing to you, HomeLane.
personalNote.intro: Before I send anything over — three things I've been thinking about.

points.title: I've been watching how you build.
points.body: The DesignCafe move — folding in a competitor instead of running at them — is the kind of move I'd want to be close to watching get made.

points.title: Your ambition is a different kind of operating problem.
points.body: ₹1,000 Cr of combined revenue across four brands, turning EBITDA positive while still designing at the individual-home level. I'd love to understand how the team keeps up.

points.title: My range might fit here.
points.body: Six years at Microsoft & Uber, then seven creative careers — mural, exhibition design, event design — where the work had to physically exist for real humans. Interior design reads like the grown-up version of that.
